steps.title: Identify the goal
steps.content: The student wants to “explain what a quenched galaxy is.” The notes state directly: “A galaxy that no longer forms stars is called a quenched galaxy.” A correct answer must include this definition. Answers that only describe events before, during, or after quenching without stating the definition fail the goal.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Before quenching, a starburst galaxy will form stars at a rapid rate.’: This describes a starburst galaxy’s behavior before quenching, not what a quenched galaxy is. Scope error: wrong galaxy, wrong phase. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘When it becomes quenched, a starburst galaxy enters the poststarburst phase.’: This describes what happens after quenching (the poststarburst phase), but never defines a quenched galaxy as one that no longer forms stars. Definition omitted. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Having entered the poststarburst phase, a quenched galaxy is one that no longer forms stars.’: This supplies the direct definition (“no longer forms stars”) and situates it in the poststarburst context — both relevant notes used. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘A starburst galaxy will lose star-forming gas and eventually become quenched.’: This describes what causes quenching, not what a quenched galaxy is. The definition is absent. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
